title: najas marina subsp. brachycarpa
intro: Najas marina subsp. brachycarpa, also known as the short-fruited water-plantain, is a sub-species of aquatic plant belonging to the Najadaceae family, native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic properties, making it effective in treating various health issues such as urinary tract infections and kidney stones. The health benefits of Najas marina subsp. brachycarpa include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and act as a natural diuretic, promoting the elimination of toxins from the body. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as an infusion or decoction, and it can be consumed as a tea or used topically as a poultice to treat various health conditions.
